externalIntegrationMustHaveCanary.rule: Any production-critical external integration (transactional email, CRM contact write, payment confirmation, webhook receiver) MUST ship with a scheduled canary that exercises its credential + happy-path within 24 hours of deploy. Detection latency target: shorter than business tolerance (LBTA = 6h).
externalIntegrationMustHaveCanary.rationale: 2026-05-13 incident: Postmark token revoked silently, all notification emails failed for unknown duration; only detected when Andrew noticed leads had stopped arriving (13+ days). vercel ls Ready + health:prod GET 200s do not detect credential rot.
externalIntegrationMustHaveCanary.enforcement: Plan-time gate: 'Risks: how do we detect silent failure of <integration>?' /compound:review reliability agent flags any new credentialed integration without a canary.

silentErrorHandlingMustHaveObservability.rule: When console.error / waitUntil / try-catch swallow patterns are the correct production behavior (don't break user flow), the same code path MUST have at least one alternate consumer (canary or test) that surfaces the swallowed error.
silentErrorHandlingMustHaveObservability.rationale: lib/leads-store.ts::storeLead is fire-and-forget by design — correct for the form path. Without a canary that writes directly with surfaced errors, an RLS / service-role / network failure goes undetected indefinitely.
silentErrorHandlingMustHaveObservability.enforcement: /compound:review reliability/correctness agent flags any new fire-and-forget integration call without an alternate-consumer test or canary.
silentErrorHandlingMustHaveObservability.reference: lib/leads-canary.ts (writes Supabase directly, not via storeLead)

prodWriteScriptMustRequireConfirm.rule: Any CLI script with a mode that mutates production external systems (email send, real contact create, payment) MUST require an explicit env-var confirmation (SMOKE_CONFIRM_PROD=yes or equivalent) when target host matches production. Refuse with non-zero exit + clear 'what would happen' message when missing.
prodWriteScriptMustRequireConfirm.rationale: 2026-05-13 review (W2): scripts/smoke-lead-canary.ts --full would have created a real AC contact + Postmark email + Notion page on every dev invocation by default. Production-default targets are a footgun.
prodWriteScriptMustRequireConfirm.enforcement: /compound:review security agent flags any new scripts/smoke-*.ts --full mode (or equivalent) that defaults to prod with no confirm gate.

dataIntegrity.rule: No hardcoded prices or duplicate program/option lists in components

apiErrorStatus.rule: API error responses use correct HTTP status
apiErrorStatus.detail: 500 for server/processing failure; 400 for invalid body/JSON

webhookAuth.rule: Webhooks require and enforce auth secret in production

modalFocusRestore.rule: Modals restore focus to trigger element on close

iconButtonA11y.rule: Icon-only buttons have aria-label or visible text

noDeadEndpoints.rule: UI must not call API routes that do not exist

reducedMotion.rule: Animated sections respect useReducedMotion(); static fallback when true

framerMotionEntranceReducedMotion.rule: Framer Motion entrance (AnimatedSection, initial/whileInView) must use useReducedMotion(); when true render plain div — JS motion not gated by CSS

jsonLdOnServer.rule: When page can be Server Component, render JSON-LD on server (schema in initial HTML)

schemaBuilderGuards.rule: Schema builders guard optional fields (image, description fallback); use env for base URL (NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL with fallback)

anchorSectionScrollMargin.rule: Anchor section targets (#hash) have scroll-mt-28 (or match nav height) when sticky in-page nav is present

noUnusedStateRefs.rule: Remove unused state and refs before merge

resizeThrottle.rule: Throttle resize listeners when used for layout (e.g. 150–200ms)

ctaConsistency.rule: Bottom CTA on program pages uses DarkSection; primary CTA styling consistent

docNonObvious.rule: JSDoc or short comment for non-obvious behavior (handleClose reset, auto-advance, reduced-motion)

brandTokens.rule: Prefer brand-* for new code; error states use brand/semantic token over raw red

parseJsonBodyAllRoutes.rule: All JSON-consuming API routes use parseJsonBody(request) then validateRequest; no inline request.json() try/catch

modalTimeoutCleanup.rule: Modals that use setTimeout/setInterval clear the timer in useEffect cleanup and in close()

modalFocusOnAutoClose.rule: When a modal closes via timeout (e.g. success), restore focus before setVisible(false)

primaryCtaBlackWhite.rule: Primary CTAs (hero, main section) use black/white or btn-primary; Sunset Cliff for accent/hover only per .cursorrules

faqAriaReducedMotion.rule: FAQ/accordion components have aria-controls, id, aria-expanded; useReducedMotion() with static fallback

closeButton48px.rule: Close buttons on modals/dialogs are at least 48×48px (min-w-[48px] min-h-[48px])

footerContrast.rule: Footer text on deep-water (or dark sections) must meet WCAG 7:1; use text-white/50 or higher for tagline and secondary text

webhookIdValidation.rule: Webhook routes must validate payload IDs (e.g. contactId) as positive integers before use in URLs or request bodies; return 400 if invalid

webhookTimingSafeSecret.rule: Webhook secret comparison must use timing-safe comparison (crypto.timingSafeEqual) with same-length buffers

heroCtaContrast.rule: Hero CTAs on dark backgrounds must have solid background (e.g. bg-white) or high-contrast text; no text-only on dark gradient

parallaxReducedMotion.rule: Parallax or scroll-driven motion must respect prefers-reduced-motion (gate like HomeHero)

pullQuoteSectionQuote.rule: Pull quotes must use PullQuote component or .section-quote class (Brand Guide gradient left edge)

decorativeSvgAriaHidden.rule: Decorative SVGs (icons in links/buttons) must have aria-hidden="true"

tailwindColorDefined.rule: Only use Tailwind color classes defined in config (no lbta-tan etc.); use lbta-beige or brand-sandstone

errorTextLbtaRed.rule: Error and required field text use text-lbta-red; error boxes use bg-lbta-red/5 border-lbta-red/20 (no raw red-*)

errorTextContrastWcagAaa.rule: Verify error text contrast for WCAG AAA (7:1) when using lbta-red on light backgrounds; use WebAIM; add darker semantic token if needed

deployIncludesGitPushAndVercelProd.rule: When user requests 'deploy' or compound:deploy, always push to GitHub and deploy to Vercel production (vercel --prod). Do not stop at preview deploy only.

heroFullHeadlineVisible.rule: Hero with bottom-pinned headline and overflow-hidden must use min-h-screen (or min-height >= content block height) so the full headline is never clipped at the top.

errorPageCtaA11y.rule: Error page (app/error.tsx) CTAs must have min-h-[48px] and visible focus ring (e.g. focus-visible:ring-2 ring-brand-victoria-cove ring-offset-2 ring-offset-white)

footerFocusRingSolid.rule: Footer (bg-brand-deep-water) interactive elements must use focus-visible:ring-white (solid), not ring-white/30, for WCAG AAA focus visibility

gradientHeroCtaFocusOffset.rule: CTAs on gradient/dark hero (e.g. contact hero) should use focus-visible:ring-offset-black/50 so focus ring offset is visible

heroHeadlineVisibleAllBrowsers.rule: Hero headline must be fully visible on load in major browsers (Chrome, Safari, Firefox); use extra top clearance (e.g. top-24 md:top-28, pt-16 md:pt-20) if headline clips in any browser

deepWaterBodyTextContrast.rule: All body and description text on bg-brand-deep-water (footer and any component in footer, e.g. NewsletterForm) must use text-white/70 or higher for WCAG 7:1

externalApiErrorLogSafe.rule: When logging third-party API errors, do not log full response body; truncate (e.g. 100 chars) or log status only to avoid PII in logs

opsCliForKeyFlows.rule: Key ops flows (lead pipeline, deploy platform) have a CLI entry point (e.g. npm run lead, npm run check:vercel) so status can be checked from the terminal without reading code

leadPipelineSmokeTest.rule: Lead pipeline has a smoke test (npm run smoke:lead) that verifies env and optional AC/GHL reachability without creating contacts; optional SMOKE_BASE_URL for live site check

formHandlerValidPayload200.rule: When request body is valid (parse + Zod pass), form handler must return 200; optional integrations (AC, GHL, storeLead) and rate limit must not cause 500 — use try/catch on rateLimit, .catch() on fire-and-forget

nextImageSizesLogos.rule: next/image for logos and fixed-size images (Header, Footer, PartnershipSection) must include sizes attribute (e.g. sizes="120px", sizes="180px") per best practice

overviewFallbacksInData.rule: Program overview 'from' price fallbacks and camp 3-day week prices must live in data (pricing-supplemental.json programsOverviewFallbacks, year2026.json threeDayFullPrice/threeDayHalfPrice); lib only reads with code fallback as last resort

rateLimitTryCatchFormRoutes.rule: Every API route that uses rateLimit() must wrap it in try/catch and allow the request on failure (fallback allowed: true) so KV outage does not cause 500

footerAllText70DeepWater.rule: All text and icon color on bg-brand-deep-water (footer, NewsletterForm, etc.) must be text-white/70 or higher; placeholders at least placeholder-white/50

scheduleDataViaProgramsData.rule: Schedule program lists must be loaded via lib/programs-data getters (getWinter2026Programs, getSpringProgramsForDisplay, etc.); no component may import season JSON (winter2026, spring-summer-2026, fall2026) directly for program list

programPriceDisplayMonthlyPreferred.rule: When a program has both pricing.monthly and session tiers (1x/2x/3x), the displayed 'from' price must prefer monthly when monthly is the minimum or primary billing (e.g. Little Stars: $120/mo not From $200/season)

webhookRateLimit.rule: Webhook routes must call rateLimit (e.g. rateLimit('webhook:'+ip, RATE_LIMITS.webhook)) with try/catch; allow request on failure. Production must return 401 when webhook secret env is unset; no user-supplied or PII in production logs.

formConfigPricingFromData.rule: Registration modal pricing must be derived from /data (programs-data, camps-data, year2026) or a single shared config; no standalone duplicate price strings in lib/form-config.ts.

interactiveButton48pxFocusRing.rule: All interactive buttons (including chat quick-reply, Send, floating toggle) must have min-h-[48px] (or 48×48) and visible focus ring (focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-sunset-cliff focus-visible:ring-offset-2)

singleTypeFromZodBoundary.rule: Data passed server→client with runtime validation must use a single type from the Zod schema (export type T = z.infer<typeof schema>); client imports that type; no 'as unknown as T' at the boundary

apiDocsBodyAndResponse.rule: API routes that expect JSON body or return non-obvious response shape (e.g. chat reply vs message) must be documented in docs/api-notes.md

formConfigLocationMatchesData.rule: lib/form-config.ts prePopulateData.location for each programId must match the program location in season JSON (winter2026, spring-summer-2026, fall2026) for that program

scheduleSlotVenueConsistent.rule: Schedule slot note and venue text in season JSON must use same wording as rest of file (e.g. 'LB High School' not 'LBHS' when others use full name)

protectedRouteSecretRequired.rule: When middleware protects a route with an env secret (e.g. /coach-hub), redirect to login or return 503 when secret unset; never allow access when unconfigured

modalOverlayFocusTrapEscape.rule: Modals/overlays must have focus trap and Escape-to-close for keyboard and screen reader users

clearCookieSecureInProd.rule: Clear-cookie header must include Secure when set-cookie used Secure (e.g. NODE_ENV === 'production') so browsers clear it over HTTPS

mobileChecklist.rule: Before shipping mobile changes: test at 320px, 375px, 768px; no horizontal scroll; primary actions ≥48×48px; anchor sections have scroll-mt when sticky nav present

formInput16pxMobile.rule: Form inputs (text, email, tel, select, textarea) must use font-size 16px (text-base or text-[16px]) on mobile to avoid iOS zoom on focus

authContractVerificationScript.rule: When a protected route has an auth API (400 invalid body, 401 wrong password), provide a runnable verification script and document how to run it (e.g. dev server with secret set, then node scripts/verify-*-auth.mjs) so validation can reach 100/100

apiValidationErrorGeneric.rule: Form/booking API 400 must not return raw validation.error (Zod field names/messages) to client; log server-side and return generic or sanitized message

modalSuccessViewFocus.rule: When a modal switches to a success view (e.g. 'Request Received'), move focus to the first focusable in that view (e.g. close button) so focus does not land on body

thankYouPageFocusRing.rule: Thank-you and confirmation pages must have visible focus ring on all focusable links (tel, mailto, Return home, app store); use brand-victoria-cove or ring-white on dark

pageSearchParamsAsyncNext15.rule: In Next 15+, page.tsx that use searchParams (or params) must treat them as Promise: async component, await searchParams, then use resolved params

contextualBookingCoachPreSelect.rule: When linking to private lesson booking from a coach context (coach bio, schedules 'Book with [Coach]'), use /book?type=private&coach=<slug>, resolve slug to display name on book page, and pass defaultCoach to the private lesson modal so the form pre-selects that coach

nextImageQualitySectionHero.rule: Section primitives and hero/bento Images using next/image should set quality={90} for consistency with project bar

chatWidgetCloseButtonA11y.rule: ChatWidget close button must have min-h-[48px] min-w-[48px] and focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-sunset-cliff focus-visible:ring-offset-2 for WCAG touch target and keyboard focus

chatWidgetSendMessageOptimized.rule: ChatWidget sendMessage useCallback should not include messages in dependency array; use functional setState to build history array from updated state

brandTokensOnly.rule: New code must use brand-* tokens (brand-pacific-dusk, brand-sandstone, etc.) instead of legacy lbta-* tokens (lbta-primary, lbta-secondary, lbta-bone) per .cursorrules Part 7

formConfigDurationFromData.rule: form-config prePopulateData.duration for programs with season dates in JSON (e.g. utr-circuit, camps) must be derived from that JSON via a helper; add test asserting duration matches season

metadataMatchesVisibleTruth.rule: export const metadata description and openGraph.description must not promise availability or outcomes the page body contradicts (thin pages, paused offerings)

pathwayPlannerNoSyntheticTuition.rule: Quizzes or planners must not display dollar amounts from client-only formulas; point to /schedules or load from shared /data

homepageHeroLcpPosterImage.rule: Homepage hero with video uses next/image priority for the poster asset; video preload=none and deferred load; fade on onPlaying; no triple fetch (layout preload + video poster + image)

thirdPartyScriptLoadStrategy.rule: Non-critical third-party scripts (analytics, pixels) default to lazyOnload for LCP; if afterInteractive is required for attribution, document the tradeoff and verify Lighthouse impact

utrPlayersAtomicBatchWrite.rule: UTR roster batch saves must execute as one atomic DB operation (single RPC) for mixed new/existing players

utrRosterNormalizedNameUnique.rule: UTR roster writes must reject duplicate normalized player names (payload and existing roster) with 409

utrColorBallBadgeIdempotent.rule: Color Ball badge saves must be idempotent for duplicate badge tokens in one payload (API dedupe + SQL DISTINCT)

supabaseMigrationRepairFirst.rule: On Supabase migration history mismatch, run targeted migration repair before db push retries (use latest CLI path)

canonicalPerPageOnly.rule: Every indexable route MUST declare its own alternates.canonical in its metadata export. The root layout MUST NOT declare alternates.canonical (avoids cascade). Pages that consolidate to another URL (landing variants -> hub) canonical to the hub intentionally.

oneRobotsTxtSource.rule: Exactly one robots.txt source per project. If app/robots.txt (or app/robots.ts) exists, delete public/robots.txt. Edit the winner. Any change to robots content gets a live HTTP smoke test post-deploy to confirm the live content matches the commit.

deployLiveSmokeTest.rule: Every compound:deploy phase ends with a live HTTP smoke test against the production domain: HTTP 200 on 5+ key routes, robots.txt content, canonical tag on at least one deep page, noindex on at least one protected route. Static prerender validation alone is insufficient.

auditClaimsVerifiedBeforeP0.rule: External audits (SEO, accessibility, performance) are treated as hypotheses, not priorities. Each flagged file must be read and the claim confirmed before being added to a P0 queue. Capture the verdict (confirmed / wrong / partial) alongside the remediation action in the plan doc.

proxyTsAwarenessNextjs16.rule: When reviewing route auth or middleware, grep for both middleware.ts AND proxy.ts at repo root. Next.js 16 renamed the file; missing this pattern can create false-positive security findings.

segmentOnlyTitlesWhenTemplateSet.rule: When app/layout.tsx declares metadata.title.template (e.g. '%s | Brand'), child pages set `title: 'Segment'` (no brand suffix) OR `title: { absolute: 'Full Custom' }` to bypass. Never include the same brand suffix the template appends — double-brand blows SERP character budget.

measureRenderedNotRaw.rule: Title and description char-length constraints (≤60, 150–160) are measured on the RENDERED <title> or <meta content="..."> tag in the static build output (`.next/server/app/*.html`) or live curl — never on the raw source string in metadata exports. Metadata templates, framework transforms, and provider interpolation can all change the rendered length.

privacyFirstContent.rule: Do not introduce family, student, or client names in new on-site content, metadata, or JSON-LD schema unless permission is documented. For testimonials, the correct substitute is NOT a visible 'Read reviews on Google' link or a star-count callout — those feel consumer-grade / Yelp, not boutique hospitality. Preferred substitutes: (a) let existing earned editorial (long-form player journeys with photography) carry the trust, or (b) quietly omit the block entirely. AggregateRating stays in JSON-LD only (invisible to users, visible to Google SERP). For bios, prefer credentials + geography. Pre-existing named content is out of scope unless a dedicated cleanup PR is explicitly requested.
privacyFirstContent.amendmentReason: Original wording suggested a live Google Reviews link as the substitute. Founder rejected that — reads as Yelp, not Aman. Corrected here.

aggregateRatingInvisibleOnly.rule: AggregateRating lives in JSON-LD only — never on the page as visible copy. Google SERP stars are a cheap ~10-20% CTR lift; on-page rating widgets and 'average of N reviews' callouts feel consumer-grade review-fishing and conflict with the LBTA / boutique aesthetic. Split: visible layer = editorial + credentials, invisible layer = schema.

brandSplitVisibleVsInvisible.rule: When a founder-directed aesthetic conflicts with a standard SEO technique, split into two layers: visible (what users read — governed by brand aesthetic) and invisible (what Google parses — JSON-LD, meta tags, OpenGraph). Both get what they need. Applies to ratings, reviews, keyword stuffing, upsell badges, and any pattern that earns SEO value but reads consumer-grade.

checkCodegenOutputBeforeReverting.rule: If `npm run ship:gate` fails the clean-tree check and reports dirty tracked files you didn't edit, ALWAYS `git diff` them before reverting. Codegen steps (tokens:build, data:sync, schema generators) may have auto-resolved canonical values across multiple files. Commit those diffs; they represent the canonical answer the repo already knows. Reverting would lose that answer and send you back to square one.

pausedEntityHiddenFlagPattern.rule: To pause (not delete) a coach, program, location, or other entity: add `hidden?: boolean` to the type, flag in JSON, filter via a private `getVisibleX()` helper used by ALL public listing/schema getters. Keep single-entity getters (getBySlug) unfiltered so direct links still resolve. Add `robots: { index: false, follow: false }` to the entity's own page with a JSDoc comment describing the 1-line reactivation path. Never delete the data for a pause that may reverse.

botKnowledgeBaseSourceLine.rule: Every Q&A in `gpt/03-KNOWLEDGE-BRAIN/23-bot-knowledge-base-2026.md` (or any future bot KB matching `gpt/03-KNOWLEDGE-BRAIN/2[0-9]-*.md`) ends with a `**Sources:**` line pointing to a JSON path or named source, AND either `**Escalate:**` (phone/email) or `**Self-serve:**` (URL). Verified by grep at acceptance time; never invent numbers — if the answer isn't in `/data/*.json` or a documented source, route to support@lagunabeachtennisacademy.com or (949) 534-0457.

founderCorrectionCrossSurfaceSweep.rule: When applying a founder correction to a fact (price, level, schedule, policy, name), grep the corrected term across `data/**/*.json` (incl. `data/coach-hub/`), `components/**/*.{tsx,ts}` (hardcoded literals), and `gpt/03-KNOWLEDGE-BRAIN/**.md` (parallel docs) BEFORE marking the loop complete. Resolve every match in the same loop. Plan should list ALL surfaces upfront via initial grep. A correction in one place that drifts in another is worse than no correction (creates contradicting truths).

numericalFactValidationScript.rule: Docs files claiming ≥10 numerical facts (prices, times, thresholds) ship with a Python or Node assertion script next to them: one assert per claim, comparing the doc's number to the source JSON. Re-run after every edit. Pattern: `data_checks = [(label, get(json,'path')==expected), ...]; report passed/failed counts`. Cheap; obvious in hindsight; saves an hour of manual cross-checking per pass. Acceptance script is the living spec.

formPipelineServerSideDedup.rule: Form-pipeline lib (storeLead-equivalent) MUST implement server-side dedup window before insert
formPipelineServerSideDedup.detail: Lookup recent (email, source) within DEDUP_WINDOW_MS (120s default); skip insert + log if found; fail-open on lookup error. Protects every route that calls the lib without per-form code changes

formPipelineClientSyncGuard.rule: Form modals SHOULD include synchronous useRef guard alongside disabled={isSubmitting}
formPipelineClientSyncGuard.detail: useState batches async; rapid double-clicks fire two onSubmits before disabled renders. Add submittingRef and check at handler entry; release in finally

multiStorageLeadAuditBeforeClaim.rule: Before answering 'are leads being saved' or 'how many leads do we have', enumerate and query EVERY storage target
multiStorageLeadAuditBeforeClaim.detail: LBTA writes to Supabase + Notion + ActiveCampaign + GoHighLevel; Meta lead-ad forms write directly to Notion via Zapier (bypasses code). Query each, dedupe by email + normalized phone, report union

deprecatedRouteReadBeforeEdit.rule: Always read an API route's source before suggesting integration edits
deprecatedRouteReadBeforeEdit.detail: Look for deprecation JSDoc, response status (200/410), redirect logic. Don't propose pipeline edits to routes returning 410 Gone

emailForbiddenHexConsolidation.rule: Tracked email templates (assets/emails/**/*.html) must not contain hex values that have been consolidated to a brand token
emailForbiddenHexConsolidation.detail: Currently #d5d1ca → lbta-stone (#E8E4DF). Adding to emailForbiddenHexes Set in scripts/check-brand-usage.ts strict-blocks the violation. WARN → ERROR in STRICT_BRAND_CHECK=1 mode.

emailCanSpamPostalAddress.rule: Customer-facing email templates must include valid physical postal address per CAN-SPAM Act §316.5
emailCanSpamPostalAddress.detail: Heuristic: any template containing "Laguna Beach" must also contain "1098 Balboa". ERROR severity (legal compliance, not style). Wired into totalErrors in scripts/check-brand-usage.ts. AC placeholder stub lbta-spring-2026.html is exempt via emailExemptFiles.

brandCheckerSeparateScannersPerDomain.rule: Adding a new file domain to the brand checker requires a separate scanner with its own scanRoot, exemptions, and rules — not appending to existing scanRoots
brandCheckerSeparateScannersPerDomain.detail: Different file types (.tsx vs .html) have different rule profiles (e.g. forbidden-fonts list flags Helvetica/Arial in React but those are required in email font-fallback stacks). Domain-aware routing at the dispatch layer prevents false positives.

testsBehaviorOverContract.rule: Tests for scanner/linter rules must exercise the exported function with synthetic fixtures, not regex-match against script source
testsBehaviorOverContract.detail: Source-grep contract tests (readFileSync + regex.match) are brittle to reformatting, type annotations, Set ordering, and accidental flags. Behavior tests via exported public API survive refactoring as long as behavior stays correct. Pre-existing "STRICT mode passes" integration test guards against scanner deletion; behavior tests guard against rule-specific regressions.

legalComplianceFailLoud.rule: Code paths that enforce legal compliance must fail-loud, never silent-skip
legalComplianceFailLoud.detail: try { ... } catch { return [] } in legal-compliance code (CAN-SPAM, GDPR, etc.) means the check could pass without ever running on a runner that lacks the precondition (e.g. git, network). Replace with console.warn + fall back to a less-precise method that still runs the check. Bias toward false-positive over silent skip.

shipGateProofInCommitBody.rule: Every commit that touches code consumed by production must record health:prod proof in the commit body
shipGateProofInCommitBody.detail: Per .cursorrules Part 15 §4. Body should include: (a) quality-gate exit, (b) test count + pass status, (c) health:prod exit + canary route count + local HEAD SHA. Reviewers can audit ship-gate compliance from git history alone.

tokenContrastVerifyBeforeLanding.rule: All new brand text tokens must verify WCAG AAA contrast (≥7:1) on morning-light, sandstone, AND white before landing
tokenContrastVerifyBeforeLanding.detail: The three light surfaces in the LBTA palette. Document hex + WebAIM ratio against each surface in the commit body. This loop's example: pacific-dusk-soft (#3D4658) — 8.71:1 on morning-light, 8.34:1 on sandstone, 9.04:1 on white. Pre-Work plan-doc review independently re-runs the contrast math; #4A5568 returned 6.62:1 on sandstone and was rejected.

clsFixReadTraceFirst.rule: All CLS fixes must read the layout-shift trace (Lighthouse 'Avoid large layout shifts' diagnostics) before fixing
clsFixReadTraceFirst.detail: Hypothesis-only fixes are blocked. Identify EVERY shift source — universal (e.g. cookie hydration) vs route-specific (e.g. useSearchParams-dependent layout chunk). This loop's example: /contact CLS=0.499 had two distinct sources (SeasonBanner ≈0.097 universal, useSearchParams ≈0.402 route-specific). Plan-only attribution to SeasonBanner alone would have left ~80% of the CLS unaddressed.

workLighthouseProductionBuild.rule: All Work-phase Lighthouse claims must be re-verified on a production build before deploy
workLighthouseProductionBuild.detail: Turbopack dev mode tanks Performance score (HMR overhead, source maps, no minification, runtime asserts). Run ./node_modules/.bin/next build then ./node_modules/.bin/next start (or measure against a real Vercel preview) before reporting Performance. Accessibility and Best Practices scores tend to hold across dev/prod; Performance does not.

previewSmokeIndependent.rule: All preview deploys must run an independent route smoke (≥6 routes) regardless of vercel ls state
previewSmokeIndependent.detail: Per the 2026-05-07 incident lesson: vercel ls Ready is necessary but not sufficient. Smoke must assert HTTP 200 + absence of x-vercel-error response header on each route. Combine with npm run health:prod (canary set) for belt-and-suspenders coverage.

tokenMigrationPlanReviewWhenLarge.rule: Mass design-token migrations across >40 files must run pre-Work plan-doc review with independent contrast verification
tokenMigrationPlanReviewWhenLarge.detail: Naïve regex sweeps blow the blast radius (this loop: 444 raw hits vs plan's 30) and risk flipping contrast on dark surfaces where opacity is intentional. STOP gate at >40 files in any single codemod batch. Use surface-aware regex with ±N-line context heuristic.

pacificDuskSoftCanonicalAAA.rule: pacific-dusk-soft (#3D4658) is the canonical AAA secondary-text token on light surfaces (LBTA-specific)
pacificDuskSoftCanonicalAAA.detail: text-brand-pacific-dusk/{30-70} utilities are scanner-warned on light surfaces (forbiddenTextOpacityOnLight category) and reserved for legitimate dark-surface use. Promote this rule to 'must' after the 6 follow-up codemod PRs land and forbiddenTextOpacityOnLight is promoted to ERROR severity in strict mode.

victoriaCoveInteractiveOnly.detail: Never use for body or static descriptive copy. Reserved for hyperlinks, focus rings, button accents, and other interactive affordances per Brand Guide.
